basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: • Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/Snowflake/ReleaseNotes.md) • There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The Snowflake CCF data connector provides the capability to ingest Snowflake [Login History Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/login_history), [Query History Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/query_history), [User-Grant Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/grants_to_users), [Role-Grant Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/grants_to_roles), [Load History Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/load_history), [Materialized View Refresh History Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/materialized_view_refresh_history), [Roles Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/roles), [Tables Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/tables), [Table Storage Metrics Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/table_storage_metrics), [Users Logs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/account-usage/users) into Microsoft Sentinel using the Snowflake SQL API. Refer to [Snowflake SQL API documentation](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/sql-api/reference) for more information. **Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:** This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in [Preview](https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/preview-supplemental-terms/) state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs: • [Microsoft Sentinel Codeless Connector Framework](https://aka.ms/Sentinel-CCP_Platform) **Data Connectors:** 1, **Parsers:** 1, **Workbooks:** 1, **Analytic Rules:** 10, **Hunting Queries:** 10 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

metadata.hidden: Hiding location, we get it from the log analytics workspace

basics.toolTip: This filters by workspaces that exist in the Resource Group selected

basics.name: workspace
basics.label: Workspace
basics.toolTip: This dropdown will list only workspace that exists in the Resource Group selected

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for Snowflake (via Codeless Connector Framework). You can get Snowflake (via Codeless Connector Framework) data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: This solution installs workbook(s) to help you gain insights into the telemetry collected in Microsoft Sentinel. After installing the solution, start using the workbook in Manage solution view.
Sets the time name for analysis
This solution installs the following analytic rule templates. After installing the solution, create and enable analytic rules in Manage solution view.

Detects possible Snowflake Discovery activity by alerting when a user account performs more than the configured
threshold of successful SHOW queries within a 5-minute window.

Detects Snowflake queries with abnormal process time when QueryTotalElapsedTime exceeds the hardcoded threshold
of 10000. Use this rule to identify unusually long-running queries that may indicate performance issues, workload abuse,
or resource exhaustion.

Detects multiple failed queries in a short timeframe. Alerts when failed Snowflake query executions for the same
TargetUsername exceed the threshold within a 5-minute bin. Create the Sentinel alert formatting only if desired with alertDetailsOverride,
customDetails, and eventGroupingSettings in the rule; keep the query logic focused on failed query volume.

elements.name: analytic4
elements.label: Snowflake - Multiple login failures by user

options.text: Detects multiple login failures by user.

elements.name: analytic5
elements.label: Snowflake - Multiple login failures from single IP

options.text: Detects Mmltiple login failures from single IP.

Detects successful DROP operations in Snowflake that may indicate destructive activity against database objects. The rule triggers when the number of successful DROP queries reaches the configured threshold within the 5-minute bin over the 1-hour query period. Investigate the TargetUsername account and the dropped object context in Snowflake audit data to determine whether the activity was authorized maintenance or malicious destruction.

Detects possible Snowflake privileges Discovery activity when a SHOW query contains both SHOW and ROLES, which
may indicate enumeration of roles and privilege structure by a user. This rule is intended to highlight Discovery behavior
associated with account and role enumeration in Snowflake.

elements.name: analytic8
elements.label: Snowflake - Query on sensitive or restricted table

options.text:
Detects successful Snowflake SELECT queries against a sensitive or restricted table. The rule evaluates Snowflake
query activity, restricts results to successful SELECT statements, extracts the referenced table name from QueryText, and
alerts when the table matches the sensitive table list defined in the query.

Detects successful Snowflake SELECT queries that begin with SELECT * and maps the executing account for SOC triage.
Use this rule to identify potentially unusual broad data retrieval behavior in Snowflake.
Detects when user asigned admin privileges.
This solution installs the following hunting queries. After installing the solution, run these hunting queries to hunt for threats in Manage solution view.

elements.name: huntingquery1
elements.label: Snowflake - Privileged users' source IP addresses

options.text: Query searches for privileged users' source IP addresses and returns associated source IPs for investigation. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)
Query searches for deleted databases. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)
Query searches for deleted tables. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)
Query searches for rarely used accounts. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)
Query searches for failed logins. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)

Query searches for Snowflake queries that consume an abnormal amount of credits, helping hunters identify potentially abusive, inefficient, or disruptive activity in the last 24 hours. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)

Query searches for Snowflake queries whose total elapsed time exceeds the hunt threshold, helping identify unusually expensive or potentially disruptive query activity. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)

Query searches for Snowflake queries classified as UNKNOWN, which may indicate anomalous, unclassified, or intentionally obscured activity. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)

Query searches for rarely used privileged users in Snowflake by identifying privileged accounts with recent query activity but no recent login activity. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)
Query searches for users' source IP addresses from successful Snowflake logins. This hunting query depends on Snowflake data connector (Snowflake Parser or Table)
